rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical cyclizative dicarboxylation of o-vinylphenyl isocyanides with CO2 to give 3-substituted 2-oxoindolin-3-acetic acids in an undivided cell with sacrificial Mg anode
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction: either the o-vinylphenyl isocyanide alkene (path A) or CO2 (path B) is reduced at the cathode to generate a carbon-centered radical anion that initiates carboxylation; Mg anode is sacrificial

key_intermediates.name: benzylic radical anion of o-vinylphenyl isocyanide (1a•-, path A)
alkyl radical 1a' after CO2 addition to alkene
dicarboxylated isocyanide intermediate (bis-carboxylate with pendant isocyanide)
seven-membered cyclic intermediate prior to 1,3-O→N acyl transfer

electron_flow: Cathode delivers one electron to either the vinyl arene (path A, favored) or CO2 (path B) to give a radical anion; this radical adds CO2 (or adds to the alkene), then a second cathodic electron transfer and protonation by H2O furnishes a dicarboxylated isocyanide that undergoes intramolecular cyclization onto the isocyanide and a 1,3-O→N acyl transfer to yield the 2-oxoindolin-3-acetic acid, while the Mg anode is oxidized as sacrificial counter-electrode.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely inhibited; starting material 1a recovered in 86%, indicating a radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace amount of product 2a observed; 2a isolated in 22% and a related product 3a in 21%, supporting radical intermediacy

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of CO2 and styrene
probe_reagents.observation: Reduction potentials are similar (·CO2-/CO2 E1/2 = -2.21 V; styrene E1/2 = -2.58 V vs SCE in DMF), so either substrate or CO2 could be reduced first, supporting two plausible pathways

probe_reagents.name: control without CO2
probe_reagents.observation: Reduced (non-carboxylated) product observed, consistent with path A involving initial cathodic reduction of the alkene

notes: Two mechanistic pathways are proposed; path A (cathodic reduction of the o-vinylphenyl isocyanide alkene first, followed by CO2 addition) is favored because in the absence of CO2 the reduced (non-carboxylated) substrate is observed. Path B (initial cathodic reduction of CO2 to ·CO2-, then addition to the alkene) cannot be ruled out. Regioselectivity for the five-membered oxindole is rationalized by preferential formation of a seven-membered ring intermediate over an eight-membered ring prior to 1,3-O→N acyl transfer.
